hypothesis: If a conditional variational autoencoder trained on Li–Si–X crystal graph embeddings and conditioned on the presence of Si and Li, target capacity >1500 mAh/g, and large stable Li-channel radius is used to design new materials, then the AI-generated silicon-based anode candidates will, after ML-based structural relaxation, exhibit capacities >1500 mAh/g and retain large continuous Li+ migration channel radii between charged and discharged states, indicating stable, high-ion-mobility anode materials.
